mentions.surface: Egnatius mihi scripsit
nam cum Silio non video confici posse
Piliae et Atticae salutem
Piliae et Atticae salutem
Publilia ad me scripsit matrem suam
cum Publilio loqueretur
Egnatius has written to me
For with Silius I do not see how it can be brought off
Greetings to Pilia and Attica
Greetings to Pilia and Attica
Publilia has written to me that her mother
having spoken with Publilius
Ciceroni velim hoc proponas
si Romae esset domumque conduceret
accommodet ad mercedes Argileti et Aventini
praestabo nec Bibulum nec Acidinum nec Messallam
praestabo nec Bibulum nec Acidinum nec Messallam
quos Athenis futuros audio
iumento certe Athenis nihil opus est
I would like you to put this to my son Cicero
if he were at Rome and were renting a house
within the rents from the Argiletum and Aventine
neither Bibulus nor Acidinus nor Messalla
neither Bibulus nor Acidinus nor Messalla
who I hear will be at Athens
Certainly there is no need of a beast of burden at Athens
